title: Expose Facial command and receipt API parity
operator_request: Facial originally exposed many workflow commands. Handshake needs native backend/API/Argus command parity so models can use the capability without file queues or external app assumptions.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Expose commands through Handshake API/client/Argus surfaces. Do not recreate .facial/data/api file queues as the primary product API unless explicitly accepted as an import adapter.

scope.summary: Create Handshake-native command routes, status APIs, receipt readers, and frontend client methods for Facial features.
base_scope: Map Facial app command surface to Handshake-native routes and client methods.
Implement list_features, start_run, get_run_status, read_artifact, identity actions, review actions, and export actions where corresponding MT backends exist.
Return structured receipts with actor, action, status, result, artifact refs, and error/degraded state.
Make async or long-running actions recoverable and observable by parallel agents.
Keep old file-queue behavior as import/reference context only unless a compatibility adapter is explicitly needed.
acceptance_criteria: Each relevant Facial command has a Handshake-native equivalent or an explicit deferred reason.
Receipts are actor/action/status/result/error structured and include artifact refs.
Artifacts are readable by ref from the backend/client path.
Async actions have recoverable status and no stranded run state after restart/reload.
Frontend client parses success, degraded, blocked, and error states without stale UI state.
risks_and_controls: Risk: routes exist but are not model-usable. Control: every route needs a client method or documented headless invocation and Argus-visible state if UI exists.
Risk: async run state is stranded. Control: run status must reconstruct from persisted artifacts/receipts.
Risk: receipt schemas drift between backend and frontend. Control: parser tests for success and error receipts.
verification: Route tests for feature listing, run start/status, artifact read, identity/review/export actions.
Frontend parser tests for receipts, degraded states, and errors.
Recovery test proving status can be rebuilt from persisted artifacts.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.
